leaderRationale: Parse failed: ERROR: codex failed: spawn codex ENOENT

conceptual_groups.summary: Output paths are relative/filename-only; canonical directory is never specified in the role definition

findings.description: The <outputs> section lists artifact filenames (e.g., '<slug>-role.md') but never specifies the canonical directory they are promoted to. The self-check correctly flags that 'tools/agent-role-builder/role/' paths are not referenced anywhere in the role markdown.

Step 5 says 'promote to canonical directory' but the canonical directory is undefined in the role. Any agent executing this role cannot determine where to write promoted artifacts without external knowledge.

Contract package_files paths are filename-only ('agent-role-builder-role.md') with no directory prefix, which is inconsistent with the self-check expectation of full paths under tools/agent-role-builder/role/.

conceptual_groups.redesign_guidance: Add a canonical output directory declaration to the <outputs> section (e.g., 'Canonical artifacts are promoted to tools/agent-role-builder/role/') and update contract package_files paths to include the full relative path from repo root.

conceptual_groups.summary: Duplicate 'Application workflow creation' entry in <scope> Not-in-scope list

findings.description: 'Application workflow creation' appears twice in the Not-in-scope list, the second instance adding a parenthetical. This creates ambiguity about whether the parenthetical is authoritative or redundant.

conceptual_groups.redesign_guidance: Deduplicate: keep the single entry with the clarifying parenthetical '(role definition markdown is a role specification, not a workflow)' and remove the bare duplicate.

conceptual_groups.summary: Step 3 self-check tag list is exhaustive but has no tolerance policy for future tag additions

findings.description: Step 3 hardcodes the exact required XML tag set. If any tag is added to the standard in the future, the self-check will incorrectly reject otherwise valid role packages. There is no stated policy for how schema evolution is handled.

conceptual_groups.redesign_guidance: Add a note that the tag set is governed by the schema version in docs/v0/architecture.md and that discrepancies trigger a pushback (not a silent pass). This aligns authority over the tag list to the architecture doc rather than hardcoding it.

conceptual_groups.summary: Round 0 unresolved pushback is not addressed in this draft

findings.description: Prior round history shows Round 0 leader=pushback with 1 unresolved issue and 0 improvements. The draft does not document what that unresolved issue was or how (if at all) it was addressed.

conceptual_groups.redesign_guidance: The decision log should carry the Round 0 pushback item and explicitly resolve or defer it with rationale before this round proceeds. If it is resolved in this draft, note which change addresses it.

residual_risks: Without a declared canonical directory in the role markdown, a new agent instance could write promoted artifacts to an incorrect location and the self-check would not catch it.
The arbitration trigger ('after 2+ consecutive splits') is stated in Step 4 but the arbitration procedure itself is not described — an implementor must infer it from external documents.
max_review_rounds=3 is in the contract but not in the role markdown; a reviewer reading only the markdown has no freeze-round ceiling.
strengths: Freeze predicate is precise and consistently stated in both <guardrails> and Step 4.
Conditional vs. always artifact distinction in <outputs> is clean and actionable.
Authority boundaries (what this tool does NOT own) are explicit and cross-reference peer tools by name.
Resume/continuation path is first-class: resume-package.json is a defined artifact with a corresponding context-gathering step.
Split-verdict and arbitration logic in Step 4 prevents silent majority-overrides of dissenting reviewers.
